title: High-Altitude Balloon Telemetry — GPS, Pressure, and Radio Downlink
content: A high-altitude balloon (HAB) flight computer logs and transmits GPS position, altitude (barometric and GPS), temperature, and pressure during ascent to ~30 km. APRS (144.800 MHz) or LoRa 433 MHz radio provides the downlink. The balloon ascends at ~5 m/s, reaching burst altitude in ~90 min; a parachute returns the payload.
extended_content: Key design considerations: (1) GPS: use a module that operates above 18 km (most civilian modules are firmware-locked at 18 km or 60,000 ft — the u-blox MAX-M8C with airborne dynamic model disabled unlocks to 80 km). (2) Pressure: BMP280 / BME280 saturates at ~300 m (10 hPa at 30 km); use for low-altitude calibration only; above 10 km rely on GPS altitude. (3) Temperature: at 30 km ambient is −40 to −60 °C; use silicone-insulated wire and keep electronics in a polystyrene box. (4) Radio: APRS uses AFSK 1200 baud on 144.800 MHz (EU) via a 300 mW transmitter (e.g., Dorji DRA818V); LoRa 433 MHz (SX1276) gives longer range (>500 km) at lower data rate. (5) Cutdown: nichrome wire through the neck string, triggered by MCU at target altitude or GPS fence. (6) Power: 2× AA lithium cells maintain voltage down to −40 °C; alkaline cells freeze and fail. Typical flight deck: Arduino Nano + NEO-M8N GPS + BMP280 + DS18B20 + LoRa SX1276. Data logged to SD card every second. Flight prediction: CUSF Predictor (habhub.org).
formula: ascent_rate [m/s] = ΔAlt / Δt;  burst_alt ≈ (V_gas / m_gas)^(1/3) × const
units: altitude: m, ascent_rate: m/s, temperature: °C, pressure: hPa
source: UKHAS (UK High Altitude Society) Technical Wiki; u-blox MAX-M8 Receiver Description; CUSF Balloon Flight Predictor
